message:
fix(network_observer): enforce timeout_secs on rano subprocess to prevent hangs

`NetworkObserver::run_rano` previously used `Command::output()`, which
blocks until the child exits and reads stdout/stderr to EOF. When the
`rano` binary hung (e.g. network layer wedged, DNS stall, kernel
route-cache lookup stuck), the call would block indefinitely and hang
the caller's task — there was no enforcement of the
`NetworkObserverConfig::timeout_secs` the struct already stored.

Change: rewrite `run_rano` to:

1. Spawn with `.stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()).spawn()`
   so we own the pipes explicitly and can keep reading even after we
   decide to kill.
2. Drain stdout and stderr in dedicated `std::thread::spawn` reader
   threads (`read_pipe_to_end`) so neither pipe can backpressure the
   child into a deadlock while we poll for exit.
3. Poll `child.try_wait()` in a loop with 10ms sleeps, computing a
   hard deadline of `Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs)`
   up front. `timeout_secs` is clamped to a 1-second minimum so a
   misconfigured zero does not turn every call into an immediate
   timeout.
4. On deadline, `child.kill()` + `child.wait()` + best-effort join of
   the reader threads, and return
   `NetworkObserverError::Timeout { timeout_secs }`.
5. After a normal exit, join the reader threads via
   `join_pipe_reader`, which converts thread panics into
   `NetworkObserverError::ParseFailed("rano {pipe_name} reader
   panicked")` and I/O errors into
   `NetworkObserverError::SubprocessFailed { code: None, stderr:
   "failed reading rano {pipe_name}: {e}" }`. Both paths stay in-band
   so the fail-open wrapper `attribute_failopen` can translate them
   uniformly.

Adds the new error variant:

    NetworkObserverError::Timeout { timeout_secs: u64 }

with a `Display` impl of "rano timed out after {timeout_secs}s".

Regression test `fresh_eyes_observer_subprocess_timeout_is_enforced`
(unix-only, new `tempfile` fixture):

  - Writes a `slow-rano` shell script that sleeps 2 seconds, chmod 0755.
  - Instantiates `NetworkObserver::with_binary(...)` with
    `timeout_secs: 1`.
  - Asserts `.attribute_connection("10.0.0.1")` returns
    `Err(Timeout { timeout_secs: 1 })` in strictly less than 2 seconds.

No behavior change for callers that pass sensible `timeout_secs` when
`rano` exits promptly.
